issue: The transcription uses a right-single-quote (stress mark) before the glottal symbol ʔ in each form, e.g. aˈʔep. The rest of the chapter never marks stress, and the combination of stress mark + glottal stop is inconsistent with the simpler [ʔ] used elsewhere (e.g. line 442, 454). This appears to be a formatting anomaly or a typo (perhaps the apostrophe was intended as a glottal stop marker and the ʔ is redundant, or vice versa).
suggestion: Either remove the stress marks (write aʔep etc.) or, if stress is genuinely being marked, add a brief note explaining the notation and ensure it is used consistently across all phonetic transcriptions in the chapter.
